D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Photoelastic Stress Fringes

squeeze a clear plastic between crossed polarizers and read stress as colour bands

Mechanical stress makes plastic birefringent. Between crossed polarizers the retardation it adds shows up as coloured isochromatic fringes — a map of internal stress.

Increase the applied load to raise the peak stress, and choose monochromatic or white light. Watch the fringe orders multiply near the loaded corner of the bracket.
